ucs.title: [LLM] Nomadic Octopus (DustSquad) — Telegram-masqueraded Delphi 'Octopus' dropper spawning hidden script host from spearphish attachment
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Nomadic Octopus / DustSquad infection chain: a Delphi-compiled 'Octopus' implant disguised as Telegram Messenger or a Central-Asian/Russian-opposition themed lure is delivered as an email attachment, dropped to a user-writable path, executed by the recipient, and immediately spawns a hidden cmd/PowerShell/wscript child to stage follow-on tooling. The combination of Telegram/CIS-political masquerade + Borland/Embarcadero (Delphi) compiler stamp + hidden-window scripting child within minutes of an email-driven file drop is uniquely characteristic of G0133 / DustSquad campaign
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\Downloads\\*","*\\Desktop\\*","*\\Public\\*") AND (Processes.parent_process_name="*telegram*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*tеlеgrаm*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*телеграм*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*opposit*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*оппозиц*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*kazakh*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*kyrgyz*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*tajik*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*uzbek*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*diplomat*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*ministry*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*embassy*") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe") AND (Processes.process="*-WindowStyle Hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-w hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-WindowStyle 1*" OR Processes.process="* /c *" OR Processes.process="* /q *" OR Processes.process="*-nop*" OR Processes.process="*-noni*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_path Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\Downloads\\*","*\\Desktop\\*") AND Filesystem.process_name IN ("outlook.exe","winrar.exe","7zG.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe") AND Filesystem.action="created" AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.scr" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*telegram*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*kazakh*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*opposit*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*оппозиц*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*телеграм*") by host Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_name as dropped_name file_hash as dropped_sha256]
| convert ctime(firstTime) as firstTime ctime(lastTime) as lastTime
| eval actor="Nomadic Octopus / DustSquad (G0133)"
| table firstTime lastTime host user actor dropped_name dropped_sha256 parent_process_name parent_process_path process_name process
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let LureKeywords = dynamic(["telegram","tеlеgrаm","телеграм","opposit","оппозиц","kazakh","kyrgyz","tajik","uzbek","ozbek","diplomat","ministry","embassy"]);
let DelphiHints = dynamic(["Embarcadero","Borland","CodeGear"]);
let UserWritable = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Desktop\", @"\Public\"]);
// 1. Inbound CIS / Telegram-themed executable attachment
let LureAttachments =
    EmailAttachmentInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileType in~ ("exe","scr","lnk","rar","zip","iso","img","msi","hta","doc","docx")
    | where tolower(FileName) has_any (LureKeywords)
    | project MailTime = Timestamp, NetworkMessageId, SenderFromAddress, RecipientEmailAddress,
              AttachmentName = FileName, AttachmentSHA256 = SHA256;
// 2. Same hash hits disk on the recipient endpoint via mail / archive / browser
let DroppedToDisk =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("outlook.exe","winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe")
    | where FolderPath has_any (UserWritable)
    | join kind=inner LureAttachments on $left.SHA256 == $right.AttachmentSHA256
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              DroppedFileName = FileName, DroppedFolder = FolderPath,
              DroppedSHA256 = SHA256, AttachmentName, SenderFromAddress, MailTime;
// 3. Delphi-compiled binary launches a hidden script host within 1h of the drop
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (UserWritable)
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (DelphiHints)
   or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoProductName has_any (DelphiHints)
   or tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) has_any (LureKeywords)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-WindowStyle Hidden","-WindowStyle 1"," /c "," /q ","-nop","-noni","-EncodedCommand","-enc ")
| join kind=inner DroppedToDisk on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId
        and $left.InitiatingProcessSHA256 == $right.DroppedSHA256
| where Timestamp between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
| project SpawnTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentFolder = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCompany = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
          ParentProduct = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoProductName,
          Child = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          AttachmentName, SenderFromAddress, MailTime, DropTime, DroppedSHA256
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let LureRegex = @"(?i)telegram|tеlеgrаm|телеграм|opposit|оппозиц|kazakh|kyrgyz|tajik|uzbek|ozbek|diplomat|ministry|embassy";
let DelphiHints = dynamic(["Embarcadero","Borland","CodeGear"]);
let UserWritable = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Desktop\", @"\Public\"]);
let ScriptHosts = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe"]);
// Stage 1 - Telegram/CIS-themed lure landing on disk via mail/archive/browser (ASIM file event)
let LureDrops =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where ActingProcessName has_any ("outlook.exe","winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe")
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (UserWritable)
    | where TargetFileName matches regex LureRegex
    | where TargetFileExtension in~ ("exe","scr","lnk","hta","msi")
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              DroppedFileName = TargetFileName, DroppedSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256;
// Stage 2 - Delphi-compiled (or Telegram-named) binary in user-writable path spawns hidden script host
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName has_any (UserWritable) or ParentProcessName matches regex LureRegex
| where (ParentProcessFileCompany has_any (DelphiHints) or ParentProcessFileDescription has_any (DelphiHints) or ParentProcessName matches regex LureRegex)
| where TargetProcessName has_any (ScriptHosts)
| where TargetProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
   or TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("-WindowStyle Hidden","-WindowStyle 1"," /c "," /q ","-nop","-noni","-EncodedCommand","-enc ")
| join kind=inner LureDrops on $left.DvcHostname == $right.DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
| project SpawnTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ParentBinary = ParentProcessName, ParentCompany = ParentProcessFileCompany,
          ChildBinary = TargetProcessName, ChildCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine,
          DroppedFileName, DroppedSHA256, DropTime
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Nomadic Octopus DustSquad - Telegram/CIS-themed Delphi binary spawns hidden script host
id: c7c1b9b0-7c2a-4d6a-b9ad-9f0c3a4d2e91
status: experimental
description: Detects Nomadic Octopus (G0133 / DustSquad) Octopus dropper behaviour - a Delphi-compiled binary masquerading as Telegram or a Central-Asian/Russian-opposition lure, executed from a user-writable path and immediately spawning a hidden cmd/PowerShell/script-host child.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0133/
  - https://securelist.com/octopus-infested-seas-of-central-asia/88200/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2018/11/20/sednit-and-fancy-bear-zebrocy/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1036
  - attack.t1564.003
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.g0133
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_path:
    ParentImage|contains:
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\Downloads\'
      - '\Desktop\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
  parent_lure:
    ParentImage|contains:
      - 'telegram'
      - 'tеlеgrаm'
      - 'телеграм'
      - 'opposit'
      - 'оппозиц'
      - 'kazakh'
      - 'kyrgyz'
      - 'tajik'
      - 'uzbek'
      - 'ozbek'
      - 'diplomat'
      - 'ministry'
      - 'embassy'
  child_script_host:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  hidden_window:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-WindowStyle Hidden'
      - '-WindowStyle 1'
      - '-w hidden'
      - '-nop'
      - '-noni'
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - ' /c '
      - ' /q '
  condition: parent_path and parent_lure and child_script_host and hidden_window
fields:
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - ParentCommandLine
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Telegram desktop installer or self-update flows that intentionally drop into AppData and spawn helper processes - exclude signed Telegram FZ-LLC binaries by signer/publisher
level: high
ucs.rationale: Generic 'office app spawns powershell' rules will not catch DustSquad cleanly because Octopus is delivered as a standalone PE rather than via macro. What IS unique to Nomadic Octopus is the precise tradecraft mix: (a) Securelist documented the malware was actively distributed during the 2018 Telegram ban in Kazakhstan disguised as a 'Russian-language Telegram' alternative, and lures repeatedly the
